title: MBTA bus crashes into parked cars, home in Medford
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: An MBTA bus crashed into parked cars and a home's steps on Playstead Road, Medford, early Sunday morning.
The 98-year-old homeowner reported the bus swerved across her yard, coming to a stop a foot from her window.
The bus driver cited 'black ice' as the cause, and the incident is under investigation by MBTA Transit Police.

title: Here are the richest people in Massachusetts, according to Forbes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Nearly 30 Massachusetts residents made the Forbes World's Billionaires List, part of a record 3,428 worldwide worth $20.1 trillion.
William “Bill” Chisholm, Boston Celtics owner, joined the billionaire ranks with a net worth of $3.2 billion.
Top Massachusetts billionaires include Abigail Johnson ($33.2 billion) and Robert Kraft ($13.8 billion), according to Forbes.

title: ‘I am a Raider’: Maxx Crosby breaks silence after Ravens trade falls through
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Baltimore Ravens canceled trade for Maxx Crosby due to concerns over his medical status, according to ESPN.
Maxx Crosby affirmed his commitment to the Raiders in a social media post after the trade fell through.
The Patriots, needing pass-rush improvements, have been mentioned as potential suitors for Crosby, who remains with the Raiders.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her grandmother, support cancer patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Laura Davis from Wakefield, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon to honor her grandmother and support Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Davis's grandmother, known as Gamma, was a significant presence in her life and battled Stage 4 pancreatic cancer until 2016.
Since 2019, Davis has run five marathons, supporting cancer charities, and the 2026 Boston Marathon will be her third World Major.

title: Carjacking suspect shot and killed in Roxbury, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston police shot and killed a carjacking suspect in Roxbury after he drove a stolen vehicle towards officers.
The incident occurred around 9:45 p.m. at 10 Linwood Square, following a carjacking report at 1500 Tremont St.
The suspect was pronounced dead at a hospital; officers involved are in good condition. Investigation by BPD and DA's office continues.

title: Colin Dorgan, who lost 3 family members in Pawtucket shooting, scores game-winning goal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Colin Dorgan scored the game-winning goal for Blackstone Valley Co-op in the Rhode Island Division 2 hockey semifinal.
The goal came weeks after Dorgan's brother, mother, and grandfather were killed in a Pawtucket shooting.
The team's coach emphasized unity and support, with players wearing patches to honor Dorgan's family members.

title: ‘We are in the midst of winter versus spring here’: Ski resorts are closing operations, reducing lift prices, limiting lifts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massachusetts and Vermont ski areas closed operations Thursday due to heavy rain and strong winds.
Wildcat Mountain reduced lift ticket prices in response to limited terrain availability during warm weather in New Hampshire.
Mount Snow in Vermont faced operational challenges from high winds and wet snow, limiting lift operations and trail access.

title: Two officers rescue woman found asleep amid house fire in Merrimac
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Merrimac police officers rescued a woman asleep during a house fire early Wednesday after a resident reported it.
Officers Paul DeCoste and Adam White responded, overcoming heavy smoke and flames to reach the sleeping resident.
Police Chief Eric Shears commended the officers for their courage and thanked the resident for calling 911.

title: A 22-year-old man died in a three-car crash in Canton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 22-year-old Marshfield man, Mathew McTiernan, died in a three-car crash on I-95 in Canton.
The crash occurred at 6:20 a.m. and involved a Toyota sedan, impacting two of three traffic lanes.
Massachusetts State Police from the Milton Barracks are investigating the cause of the crash.

title: Baby boomers feeling crushed by housing crunch
author: Lindsay Crudele
bullets: Baby boomers, representing 53% of home sellers in 2025, face increasing housing uncertainty due to the wealth gap.
Southbridge complex residents, mostly seniors, face rent hikes up to 70% after Shorelight Real Estate's acquisition.
Massachusetts sees a rising 65+ population, now 23%, doubling since 2010, leading to complex housing challenges.
